prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: 3 out of 5 stars. Waking Up arrives daily without fail, though delivery times fluctuate and rarely align with stated preferences. Setup is manual, typically requiring an alarm, ambient light, or external prodding; performance improves when paired with caffeine. Initial boot is inconsistent: some units load clear focus in under five minutes, others present extended buffering with sandpaper eyes and limited motor coordination. Included features (awareness, basic mobility, calendar recall) function as described, but secondary modules—mood stabilization, optimism—are unreliable at launch and may require updates over the first hour. Snooze integration is poorly implemented and often triggers repeat alerts without meaningful improvement. Battery reporting is inaccurate, oscillating between apparent full charge and sudden depletion by midafternoon. Build quality varies by previous-day usage; overuse reduces morning responsiveness. Documentation is sparse; user must rely on habit. Not fully compatible with early meetings or bright enthusiasm. Still, given the lack of viable alternatives and seamless bundling with the rest of the day, it represents acceptable value. Would continue using because it is effectively mandatory; recommend with caveats to existing users only.
